rationale: The citing article is a broad conceptual editorial that narratively reviews decades of mitigation-related JMR work, so individual citations may be either substantive exemplars or brief background mentions. Here, the matched evidence is only a single DOI-level reference with no in-text citation context, quotation, or reference details beyond the DOI. Because the cited paper is plausibly relevant to mitigation in health behavior (detection/prevention mechanisms) but the current evidence is too sparse to confirm substantive use, this pair is uncertain.
